channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Instagram’s New Instants App Is a Snapchat Clone for Thirst Traps
item.description: Instagram’s Instants app lets you send disappearing photos—and it’s probably where your horny friends will post spicy pics.

item.title: AMD’s best CPU tech for gamers is coming to workstations too
item.description: For the first time, AMD is including its 3D V-Cache tech in its commercial workstation processors with a refreshed line of Ryzen PRO 9000 series chips. Up until now, AMD's 3D V-Cache chips have mainly been geared toward gamers, but they've also gotten better at creative tasks over recent years. In our review of the […]

item.title: Apple may open up the App Store to agentic AI
item.description: But doing so could threaten its bottom line.

item.title: Your Steam Controller might scream if you drop it
item.description: A Wilhelm one, no less.

item.title: The scariest part of LinkedIn’s layoffs isn’t the number of jobs cut
item.description: The professional networking giant is downsizing during one of its strongest financial periods in years. LinkedIn on Wednesday joined what’s become a near-daily drumbeat of layoff announcements among tech companies.

item.title: Frontier AI models don't just delete document content — they rewrite it, and the errors are nearly impossible to catch
item.description: As large language models become more capable, users are tempted to delegate knowledge tasks where models process documents on their behalf and provide the finished results. But how far can you trust the model to stay faithful to the content of your documents when it has to iterate over them across multiple rounds?A new study by researchers at Microsoft shows that large language models silently corrupt documents that they work on by introducing errors. The researchers developed a benchmark that simulates multi-step autonomous workflows across 52 professional domains, using a method that automat...

item.title: Trump Mobile CEO says the phones are shipping. The terms and conditions say it doesnt have to.
item.description: The Trump Mobile CEO says the phones are finally shipping. But the terms and conditions make one thing perfectly clear: It doesn't have to!

item.title: iBuyPower slashes up to $350 off gaming PCs powered by a 16GB RTX 9070 XT, Ryzen 7 7800X3D and 32GB RAM in its Memorial Day sale
item.description: The iBuyPower Memorial Day sale is one you don't want to miss out on. We've highlighted one particularly good deal out now.

item.title: X has a new private hub for users' bookmarks, likes, articles and long videos
item.description: The history tab might be the most useful feature X has added in forever.

item.title: 'A decade or so away, we’ll be viewing it as a more normal way to build data centers': Google and SpaceX are reportedly in talks to build data centers in orbit
item.description: There's more movement on the data-centers-in-space front, as Google and SpaceX are apparently teaming up.

item.title: Meta AI is coming to Threads, and some users aren’t thrilled
item.description: Threads is testing a feature that lets users summon Meta AI into posts and replies for real-time context, though many users say they never asked for it. Threads is rolling out Meta AI, which will provide real-time context when mentioned in a post or reply.

item.title: We know whats making your phones battery drain so quickly
item.description: CNET experts explain why phone batteries drain quickly, how lithium-ion batteries degrade, and tips to improve battery health.

item.title: More companies are offering a simple workplace perk to make commutes less painful as gas prices soar
item.description: Ridepanda, which allows companies to offer e-bike subscriptions, has seen a spike since the Iran war began. In Seattle, the average price of a gallon of gas is now $5.96, a 30-cent increase from only a month ago and a $1.50 increase from a year ago.

item.title: Musk’s xAI is running nearly 50 gas turbines unchecked at its Mississippi data center
item.description: Gas turbines at xAI's Colossus 2 data center have drawn a lawsuit over the company's use of "mobile" gas turbines as power plants.

item.title: Amazon workers are under pressure to up their AI usage—so they’re making up extraneous tasks
item.description: In a new report, employees say Amazon tracks their consumption of ‘AI tokens,’ and they’ve been creating unproductive AI agents just to eat them up. According to Amazon employees, the company is pushing them to incorporate more and more AI in their workflows. What exactly they should be using it for is less clear—leaving the door open for employees to waste AI resources on unnecessary tasks....

item.title: Google, Box CEOs say this is the “most in-demand” job in tech
item.description: Google is planning to fill hundreds of these positions, while Anthropic and OpenAI are also expanding their use of the role. In 2011, Palantir created a combined role for their solutions engineers and integration engineers. The company called this new role “forward-deployed engineers,” or FDEs, for short. An Andreessen Horowitz blog post dubbed the recasting “title arbitrage,” arguing that Palantir had created this new title to signify the important, new capabilities and powers evolving at the company. Put simply: FDEs are people who can sell AI products to businesses while also teaching AI mo...

item.title: Alibaba’s AI and cloud revenue jump 38%
item.description: The Chinese e-commerce company’s U.S.-traded shares rose more than 7% after Wednesday’s earnings announcement. China’s Alibaba said that growth accelerated for both its artificial intelligence and cloud businesses in the latest quarter, driven by the AI boom, even though overall revenue rose just 3% to 243 billion yuan ($36 billion).

item.title: Anthropic’s Cat Wu says that, in the future, AI will anticipate your needs before you know what they are
item.description: The head of product for Claude Code and Cowork says that the next big step for AI is proactivity.

item.title: From F1 sets to new Botanicals plants and a massive Lord of the Rings set, these are the best new Lego sets of May
item.description: We rounded up the best new Lego sets available to purchase as of May 13, including a Lewis Hamilton helmet and Cristiano Ronaldo themed-set.

item.title: Bad news employee — most executives admit using AI makes them value human workers less
item.description: After using AI, many business leaders say are less likely to value their human workers, but the technology is still failing to deliver consistent ROI.

item.title: The crypto Clarity Act returns to the Senate this week. The banks are already trying to kill it.
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, the newsletter for Verge subscribers that goes into tech shenanigans that take place in the backrooms of Washington. Really, it sometimes does feel like the online series The Backrooms: a parallel universe with no internal logic, evil corporations lurking in the background, and mind-rending eldritch horrors around every corner. (Not […]...

item.title: The Talos Principle 3 will wrap up the series
item.description: Devolver promises "both familiar and all-new mechanics."

item.title: What It Will Take to Make AI Sustainable
item.description: Researcher Sasha Luccioni argues we need better emissions data and a better sense of how people are using AI in the first place.

item.title: Fighting Trump will make or break Disney’s new CEO
item.description: A week ago, newly appointed Disney CEO Josh D'Amaro was busy regaling investors with plans to turn Disney Plus into the company's "digital centerpiece." By last Friday, though, his attention had presumably shifted to a fight with the Trump administration over free speech. Disney-owned ABC has now accused the administration of violating its First Amendment […]...

item.title: Les mystérieux flashs bleus ultra-rares dans l’Univers cacheraient une destruction stellaire terrifiante
item.description: Les astrophysiciens continuent de découvrir des phénomènes inédits et mystérieux dans le cosmos, comme les Luminous Fast Blue Optical Transients (LFBOT) révélés depuis moins d'une décennie. On sait peut-être enfin ce qui se cache derrière ces événements transitoires optiques bleus rapides...

item.title: DHS Plans Experiment Running ‘Reconnaissance’ Drones Along the US-Canada Border
item.description: Autonomous drones and ground vehicles will stream “battlefield intelligence” over 5G along the US-Canada border in a bilateral DHS experiment this fall.

item.title: Andreessen Horowitz is the midterm elections' biggest donor
item.description: The venture capital firm has reportedly donated $115.5 million during this election cycle.

item.title: Netflix misspelled a lead Devil May Cry character's name and had to recall official merchandise
item.description: The t-shirt in question refers to Dante and "Virgil."
